title: CINF or RE: Which P&C Insurance Stock is Better Positioned?
text: Let's see how Cincinnati Financial (CINF) and Everest Re (RE) fare in terms of some of the key metrics.

title: Using Return On Capital Employed To Generate Alpha With Dividend Aristocrats
text: Return on Capital Employed (ROCE) is a financial ratio that measures a company's profitability and efficiency in utilizing its capital to generate returns. Strategies that focus on investing in companies with high ROCE or significant improvements in ROCE have historically outperformed the overall market. ROCE should be incorporated into stock research as it is an important metric for evaluating the long-term performance of a business.

title: Want Safe Income? This Stock Raised Its Dividend In the Last 9 Recessions
text: Cincinnati Financial has increased its dividend every year since 1960. The property and casualty insurer has displayed strong underwriting and excellent cash management during lean times to keep its dividend growing.

title: Here's Why Investors Should Hold Cincinnati Financial (CINF)
text: Cincinnati Financial (CINF) stands to gain from price increases, higher renewal written premiums, growth initiatives and improvement in equity portfolio dividends and interest income.

title: 4 Dividend Aristocrat Buys For June And 5 To Watch
text: "Dividend Aristocrats in the S&P 500 Index have raised their annual payouts every year for at least 25 consecutive years. title: My 12 Best Dividend Kings For June 2023
text: The Dividend Kings are an elite group of companies that have paid and increased dividends for at least 50 consecutive years. The strategy focuses on selecting the top quarter of Dividend Kings with the best-forecasted EPS growth rate combined with the current valuation. A buy-and-hold approach is recommended for this strategy, as it is easier and more tax-friendly.
